---
title: "Your Paper Title"
subtitle: "Preliminary draft --- please do not cite without permission"
author:
  - name: Your Name
    email: you@example.org
    affiliations:
      - name: Your Department, Your University
        address: Some Address, Country
        corresponding: true
  - name: Co-Author One
    email: one@example.org
    affiliations:
      - name: Co-Author Department, Co-Author University
        address: Some Address, Country
date: today
date-format: "[This revision:] D MMMM YYYY"
abstract: |
  A short abstract goes here. Summarize the paper's question, data,
  method, and main findings in 150--250 words. Replace this text with
  your own.

  \bigskip
  \noindent\textbf{Keywords:} keyword one, keyword two, keyword three

  \noindent\textbf{JEL Classification:} A00, B00, C00
keywords:
  - keyword one
  - keyword two
  - keyword three
thanks: |
  We thank seminar and conference participants for helpful comments.
  Replace this acknowledgments block with your own.
bibliography: references.bib

format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    documentclass: article
    classoption:
      - "12pt"
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    linestretch: 2
    indent: true
    fontsize: 12pt
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: red
    citecolor: blue
    urlcolor: magenta
    cite-method: natbib
    biblio-style: apalike
    keep-tex: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        % Quarto's default template already loads amsmath, amssymb,
        % booktabs, graphicx, caption, and hyperref. The extras below
        % are what a minimalist economics/finance paper typically adds.
        \usepackage{amsthm}
        \usepackage{subcaption}
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \AtBeginEnvironment{tabular}{\onehalfspacing}
        \tolerance=1
        \emergencystretch=\maxdimen
        \hyphenpenalty=10000
        \hbadness=10000
        \theoremstyle{plain}
        \newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
        \newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}
        \newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
        \theoremstyle{definition}
        \newtheorem{definition}[theorem]{Definition}
        \newtheorem{assumption}[theorem]{Assumption}
---

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

A paragraph or two of motivation: what question does this paper ask,
why does it matter, what's the contribution, what's the punchline?
Conclude with a roadmap of the remaining sections.

# Data {#sec-data}

Describe the sample, sources, and any cleaning. Reference Table 1
([@tbl-summary]) for summary statistics.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Summary statistics."
#| echo: false
#| eval: false
# knitr::kable(your_data_frame, booktabs = TRUE)
```

# Methodology {#sec-method}

State the model or estimator. A representative equation:

$$
y_{it} = \alpha_i + \beta\, x_{it} + \varepsilon_{it}
$$ {#eq-baseline}

@eq-baseline is the baseline specification. Identifying assumptions
go in the prose below.

::: {.proof}
Sketch the argument or refer to an online appendix.
:::

# Results {#sec-results}

Lead with the main result. Discuss robustness in subsections.

## Robustness {#sec-robust}

Describe alternative specifications, placebo tests, etc.

# Conclusion {#sec-conc}

Summarize the contribution, acknowledge limitations, and point to
follow-up questions.
